text: Constructing gene regulatory networks from transcriptomic data often requires extensive datasets of gene expression to deduce potential regulatory linkages, which makes inference challenging in conditions where data is limited, such as rare diseases and in rare or hard to obtain samples such as diseased tissues. Transfer learning, a subfield of deep learning, which was demonstrated in domains like natural language understanding1,2 and computer vision3 to train models on a large benchmark of common data that can be fine-tuned to many unique tasks for which data is rare, stands to greatly aid our ability to infer network connectivity. In this study, we propose a novel context dependent deep learning framework model Geneformer, which is pre-trained on a Genecorpus-30M (~30M) of single cell data to provide knowledge on the dynamics of biological networks with great detail. Upon being trained, the model uses this information to gain insight in the structural hierarchy in the Genecorpus and represents it through attention weights and hence facilitates inference with limited datasets in the network biology domain. Funding National Institutes of Health Grant 1U19AI135972 to XSL, grant R01NS085251, and by a Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH) Departmental Startup fund to PTE; the Sarnoff Center for Artificial Vision Research and Training of the Ohio Board of Regents, grant OBR ARCTransport: RDOH005013 “Development of Geneeditor gene editing cell paint technologies and the Biohacked Smart Microtissues for drug discovery,” the Kusmer Foundantion and the Schwartz Family Foundation (to AC). ZRAS and MCH were supported by NIH R01CA243583, NIH P30 CA030199, NSF CAREER 2047350, a Stanford Dean’s Research Award, and by the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation. This publication is made possible by Award Number R01NS115817 to HM, and is part of a project entitled ‘Investigating Neurovascular dysfunction in Autism Spectrum Disorders’. Its contents are solely the responsibility of the authors and do not necessarily represent the official views of Awarding organizations. Also supported by 5T32GM07270 and by an MGH Departmental Startup Fund (EMB). CVT conceived of the work, developed Geneformer, assembled Genecorpus-30M, and designed/performed computational analyses. LX, AC, ZRAS, MCH, HM, and EMB performed experimental validation in engineered cardiac microtissues. MDC performed pre-processing, cell annotation, and differential expression analysis of the cardiomyopathy dataset. ZZ provided data from the TISCH database for inclusion in Genecorpus-30M. XSL and PTE designed analyses and supervised the work. CVT, XSL, and PTE wrote the manuscript. All authors edited the manuscript. XSL conducted this work while on faculty at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute and is currently a board member and CEO of GV20 Oncotherapy. PTE has received sponsored research support from Bayer AG, IBM Research, Bristol Myers Squibb, and Pfizer. PTE has also served on advisory boards or consulted for Bayer AG, MyoKardia, and Novartis. AC is an employee of Bayer
